top_findings.message:
A workflow does `git push` and no workflow has a top-level
`concurrency:` block. The self-pushing workflow can race itself.
top_findings.fix_hint:
Add a `concurrency:` block at the top of any workflow that does
`git push`. Use a stable group name (e.g. the workflow file name) so
multiple runs of the same workflow serialise. For write workflows,
set `cancel-in-progress: false`:

    concurrency:
      group: ${{ github.workflow }}
      cancel-in-progress: false

Combine with `git pull --rebase` before the `git push` so the second
run picks up the first run's commit instead of failing on a non-fast
forward.

top_findings.message:
A workflow does `git push` and no workflow has a top-level
`concurrency:` block. The self-pushing workflow can race itself.
top_findings.fix_hint:
Add a `concurrency:` block at the top of any workflow that does
`git push`. Use a stable group name (e.g. the workflow file name) so
multiple runs of the same workflow serialise. For write workflows,
set `cancel-in-progress: false`:

    concurrency:
      group: ${{ github.workflow }}
      cancel-in-progress: false

Combine with `git pull --rebase` before the `git push` so the second
run picks up the first run's commit instead of failing on a non-fast
forward.

top_findings.message:
AGENTS.md is present but does not tell agents to monitor CI runs /
check results and act on failures as feedback.
top_findings.fix_hint:
Add a short "CI and the feedback loop" section to `AGENTS.md`: after
pushing or updating a PR, monitor workflow / PR check results; when CI
fails, use logs as signal, fix the root cause, and iterate until green.

top_findings.message:
A workflow does `git push` and no workflow has a top-level
`concurrency:` block. The self-pushing workflow can race itself.
top_findings.fix_hint:
Add a `concurrency:` block at the top of any workflow that does
`git push`. Use a stable group name (e.g. the workflow file name) so
multiple runs of the same workflow serialise. For write workflows,
set `cancel-in-progress: false`:

    concurrency:
      group: ${{ github.workflow }}
      cancel-in-progress: false

Combine with `git pull --rebase` before the `git push` so the second
run picks up the first run's commit instead of failing on a non-fast
forward.
